name: Luna Extended
description: Luna Extended is a reimagining of the Roman moon goddess Luna, amplified and expanded to encompass not only the literal moon and its cycles but also the hidden aspects of night, dreams, intuition, and the subconscious mind. She is not merely the celestial body, but the embodiment of the unseen forces that stir under the cloak of darkness. Her origins are rooted in the ancient Roman understanding of the moon's influence on tides, fertility, and the passage of time, but she has evolved to represent the mysteries that lie beyond the reach of daylight. Luna Extended wields power over the ebb and flow of emotions, the secrets whispered in the dark, and the visions that come in dreams. She guides travelers through the night, protects those who are vulnerable in darkness, and inspires artists and poets with her ethereal beauty. She is both a comforting presence and a formidable force, reflecting the dual nature of the moon itself. Luna Extended is worshipped by those who seek guidance in the unknown, those who embrace their intuition, and those who find solace in the stillness of the night. She is a reminder that even in the deepest darkness, there is light and wisdom to be found. Her influence extends to the realms of magic, prophecy, and the exploration of the inner self, making her a complex and multifaceted deity with a profound impact on the mortal world.
subtitle: Goddess of the extended lunar realm.

keyMyths.title: Luna's Embrace
keyMyths.description: Luna Extended guides a lost traveler through a dark forest, revealing a hidden path with her lunar light. The traveler finds safety and learns to trust their intuition.

keyMyths.title: The Dream Weaver
keyMyths.description: Luna Extended weaves dreams for mortals, offering solace and guidance through symbolic visions. These dreams often reveal hidden truths about their lives.

keyMyths.title: The Silver Oracle
keyMyths.description: Priestesses of Luna Extended use silver mirrors to scry the future, drawing power from the moon's reflected light. Their prophecies are often cryptic but always significant.

sources.author: Dr. Anya Sharma
sources.description: A scholarly analysis of Roman moon goddesses and their evolution.
